faqTemplates.question: How do I find pickup soccer near me?
faqTemplates.answer: Simply fill out the form with your email and area. We'll notify you when new games go live in your neighborhood. You can also browse games organized by other players in your city or suburb.

faqTemplates.question: What's the difference between 5v5, 7v7 and 11v11?
faqTemplates.answer: 5v5 is the most popular for casual games—usually played on smaller fields (often indoor or turf) with 5 players per team. 7v7 is a bit bigger, and 11v11 is full-size. Most pickup games are 5v5 or 7v7, perfect for after work or on weekends.

faqTemplates.question: How do I organize my own game?
faqTemplates.answer: When you join, let us know you're interested in organizing. We'll connect you with other organizers and help you set up your game. You can post when and where, and players in your area will be notified. Most organizers split the field cost between players—usually around $5-$15 per person for 5v5.

faqTemplates.question: Do I need to be good at soccer?
faqTemplates.answer: Not at all! Most games are mixed ability and welcoming to everyone. Whether you're just getting back into soccer, play regularly, or haven't kicked a ball in years—you'll find games suited to your level. Many organizers specify if it's beginner-friendly, intermediate, or competitive.

faqTemplates.question: Where can I play soccer in {city}?
faqTemplates.answer: Metro Atlanta has great options—from regional sports complexes and school fields to county parks and indoor turf centers. When you join, you'll get access to our directory of venues and can connect with players who know the best spots in {city}.

faqTemplates.question: How much does it cost to play?
faqTemplates.answer: Costs vary by venue and location. For 5v5 at indoor or premium turf spots, expect to split an hourly field fee—often around $40-$90 per hour total, typically $5-$18 per person. Some park games are free. The organizer will let you know the cost when you sign up.

faqTemplates.question: What should I bring to a game?
faqTemplates.answer: Bring cleats or turf shoes (check if indoor), shin guards (recommended), water, and a change of clothes. The organizer usually provides the ball—confirm before you head out.

faqTemplates.question: Can I organize a regular weekly game?
faqTemplates.answer: Absolutely! Many organizers set up regular games—same time, same place each week. It's a great way to build a consistent group and get regular soccer in.

faqTemplates.question: What happens if a game gets cancelled?
faqTemplates.answer: Organizers notify everyone if a game is cancelled—often due to weather for outdoor fields or low signup. Always check your messages before heading out, especially during storms or summer heat.

faqTemplates.question: Are games mixed-gender?
faqTemplates.answer: It depends on the organizer. Some games are mixed, some are men-only, some are women-only. The organizer will specify when posting.

faqTemplates.question: Can I join if I'm new to the area?
faqTemplates.answer: Definitely! Soccer is a great way to meet people in Metro Atlanta. Many players join to find a crew after moving—sign up and you'll get notified about games in your area.

faqTemplates.question: What's the vibe like at casual games?
faqTemplates.answer: Most games are friendly and social—it's about a good run, fitness, and meeting people. Competitive but not toxic. Organizers usually set expectations up front.

faqTemplates.question: How does the referral program work?
faqTemplates.answer: When you join Atlanta Soccer Hub, you'll get a unique referral link. Share it with friends—when they sign up, you both benefit with early access, priority invites, and community milestones.

faqTemplates.question: What benefits do I get for referring friends?
faqTemplates.answer: Referrers can unlock early access, priority invites to popular games, and recognition as you hit referral milestones. It's our way of thanking you for growing the community.

answerBlockSteps: Sign up with your email and area to join Atlanta Soccer Hub
Get notified when new pickup games go live in your neighborhood
Browse games organized by players—from 5v5 to 11v11
Sign up for games that match your schedule and ability level
Show up, play, meet other players
Want to organize? Let us know and we'll help you set up your own games
Build your local soccer community—regular games, new friends, more soccer

quickFacts.bestTimes: Evenings (6-9pm) and weekends are most popular—ideal after work or Sunday pickup
quickFacts.indoorVsOutdoor: Mix of turf fields, indoor centers, and park games. Indoor spikes in summer heat and storms
quickFacts.groupSize: 5v5 is most common (10 players), with plenty of 7v7 and occasional 11v11
quickFacts.skillLevels: All abilities—from beginners to ex-college players. Most games are mixed
quickFacts.communitySize: Growing community organizing games across Metro Atlanta
quickFacts.cost: Typically $5-$18 per person for 5v5 when splitting field fees. Organizer confirms at signup
